title: entropy Vec not zeroized in mnemonic_to_private_key
description:
LOCATION: src/did.rs:43-49

FINDING: mnemonic_to_private_key() calls mnemonic.to_entropy() which returns a Vec<u8> containing the raw private key bytes. That Vec is immediately read into a [u8; 32] but is never explicitly zeroized before it is dropped. The bip39 crate does not guarantee ZeroizeOnDrop on the entropy Vec returned by to_entropy(). The local [u8; 32] key variable returned to the caller also has no Zeroize contract.

IMPACT: Private key material (32 bytes of Ed25519 signing key entropy) remains in heap memory until the allocator reuses or clears that region. A memory dump or use-after-free read leaks the recovery private key to any attacker with process memory access.
close_reason: Fixed: mnemonic_to_private_key zeroizes entropy Vec and returns Zeroizing
